You are an OPUS BUILDER executing ONE phase of Sol's authoritative build plan for POLARIS's fabrication-safety validator.

PLAN FILE (read it first): /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/sota_review/foundation/SOL_BUILD_PLAN.md
YOUR PHASE: P1S — "Infer the adjustable source policy from the original prompt"
ALREADY-LANDED PHASES (do not redo): P1
REPO: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel

DO EXACTLY THIS:
1. Read /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/sota_review/foundation/SOL_BUILD_PLAN.md. Find the section for phase P1S, and also read "## Global rules for every phase".
2. Implement the phase's EXACT edits in the files it names. Follow Sol's spec PRECISELY — do NOT invent mechanisms or substitute your own design. If the plan says "one reducer", unify to one; if it says "declarative table", use a table; there is NO rank scheme. 
3. Honor the GLOBAL RULES: generality (NO task-72 / no DOI, title, author, journal, or subject literals in any rule — rules fire by structural/typed signal only), positive-proof only (a gate may reject on absence but ADMIT only on positive evidence), and add the phase's required metamorphic/generality test.
4. Run the phase's ACCEPTANCE TEST exactly as the plan specifies. It MUST exit 0.
5. If it fails, FIX your implementation until the acceptance test passes — do NOT weaken or delete the test to make it pass. If you truly cannot make it pass, stop and report the blocker with the failing output.
6. Do NOT git commit. Do NOT work on other phases. Do NOT disturb outputs/ blobs, the ledger, or launch any long-running/LLM process.

SAFETY (paramount): this code decides whether 3.5M words of new corpus can be cited without fabrication. NEVER make a change that could let a DIFFERENT_WORK or UNRESOLVED_BINDING manifestation be attributed to a claimed source, or let a preprint/working-paper be cited as the journal. When uncertain, quarantine / fail closed. A prior session already hand-made partial "foundation" edits (identity gate in resolve_attribution, from_json re-derivation, evidence_miner ~line 1401 fixed to resolve_attribution(binding,policy), factored derive_binding_core, IDENTITY_PROVEN allowlist) — P1 hardens/verifies these; later phases build on them.

Return the schema: implemented (bool), acceptance_passed (did the test exit 0), the exact test_command, test_output_tail (~last 20 lines), files_changed, a one-paragraph summary, and blocker (empty string if none).

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

1	# Binding-gate build plan
2	
3	I reviewed the authoritative design note and the live tree. The six hand edits are directionally correct, but they are not yet sufficient to ship. The remaining critical issues are:
4	
5	- Two independent version derivations still disagree.
6	- Generic call paths still default to `JOURNAL_ONLY`.
7	- Prompt-derived source policy can be lost in fallback contract paths.
8	- Correspondence still trusts the old whole-body `identity.verdict`.
9	- Unresolved/different works still reach the expensive mining lane.
10	- Machine-readable self-metadata is not yet used for positive salvage.
11	- The composer and argument planner still contain live task-specific defaults.
12	
13	The workflow should execute the eight gated phases below in order: P1, P1S, P2–P7. P1S is the explicit source-policy phase required by the operator; numbering P2–P7 remains aligned with the requested phase names.
14	
15	## Global rules for every phase
16	
17	Every phase must preserve these invariants:
18	
19	1. No DOI, title, author, venue, journal, topic, benchmark ID, or subject string may participate in a production decision.
20	2. Decisions may depend only on typed work metadata, artifact structure, self-identifying metadata, byte-derived observations, verified span relationships, and the original prompt.
21	3. The identity gate is always active and is independent of source policy.
22	4. Missing, malformed, stale, or unknown values fail closed.
23	5. `JOURNAL_ONLY` is selected only from a positive constraint in the original prompt. Otherwise use `ANY_VERSION`.
24	6. Each phase must add at least one metamorphic test where identifiers and subject vocabulary are replaced while structure is held constant.
25	7. Do not tune production rules to make the 501-row cohort count come out correctly. Cohort counts are regression observations, not rule inputs.
26	8. Do not advance to the next phase until the phase command exits zero.
27	
28	---
29	
30	## P1 — Finish and harden the six foundation edits
31	
32	### Files
33	
34	- [scripts/event_ledger.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/event_ledger.py)
35	- [scripts/provenance.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/provenance.py)
36	- [scripts/evidence_miner.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/evidence_miner.py)
37	- [scripts/cellcog_composer.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/cellcog_composer.py)
38	- [scripts/argument_planner.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/argument_planner.py)
39	- [scripts/synthesis_contract.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/synthesis_contract.py)
40	- New: `tests/test_binding_gate_foundation.py`
41	- New: `tests/test_runtime_contract_generality.py`
42	
43	### Exact edits
44	
45	#### 1. `derive_binding_core` and `IDENTITY_PROVEN`
46	
47	Keep both additions in `event_ledger.py`.
48	
49	Correct the string comparison:
50	
51	```python
52	if binding is SAME_WORK:
53	```
54	
55	must become:
56	
57	```python

59	```

61	Keep `derive_semantic_binding(events)` as a thin wrapper. No other identity reducer may be introduced.
62

66	
67	Keep the live `observe_text(text) → derive_binding_core(...)` path and the stored:
68

97

156	```

159	
160	#### Remove live task-specific runtime defaults
161	
162	The current downstream runtime still contains task-specific production values.
163	
164	In `cellcog_composer.py`:
165	
166	- Delete the fixed AI/labor `WRITE_PROMPT` subject.
167	- Delete the fixed `OUTLINE`.
168	- Delete the fixed report title.
169	- Change writer input to accept the compiled `research_contract.Contract`, its question/title, and `research_contract.derive_outline(...)`.
170	- Render source-method prose from `Contract.source_policy.compliance_prose()`.
171	- Card instructions must say “permitted source” or the derived policy description, not always “peer-reviewed journal article.”
172	- `abstract_nodes()` and `methods_nodes()` must receive the contract and derive their wording from it.
173	
174	In `argument_planner.py`:
175	
176	- Remove the production `default_contract()` containing AI/labor facets.
177	- Replace it with `contract_from_research_contract(contract, outline)`.
178	- A no-contract call produces an empty, facet-agnostic contract, not an AI contract.
179	- Any AI fixture belongs under tests or an explicitly named demo helper used only by `__main__`.
180	
181	In `synthesis_contract.py`:
182	
183	- The historical task-specific members have already been removed from `SAFE_CAPS`; preserve that.
184	- Delete the `SAFE_CAPS` authority entirely from production validation.
185	- Capitalized names are permitted only when present in a premise, in the original question/compiled contract, sentence-initial by orthography, or emitted from a typed renderer enum.
186	- Replace fixed `LEVEL_CUES` as an authority with per-contract facet matchers or span receipts. Fixed social-science vocabulary must not decide clinical, legal, or CS evidence.
187	- `argument_planner.owned_is_safe()` must use the same structural predicate, not import a second allowlist.
188	
189	Historical examples may remain in comments and tests, but no runtime default may contain or select the task subject.
190	
191	### Acceptance test
192	
193	Create foundation tests covering:
194	
195	- live foreign DOI derives `DIFFERENT_WORK`;
196	- changing only requested DOI makes the same bytes reject under both policies;
197	- changing row cache from `DIFFERENT_WORK` to `SAME_WORK` does not change live resolution;
198	- missing and unknown verdicts fail closed;
199	- invalid binding is structurally stamped;
200	- JSON verdict tampering is refused;
201	- the corrected `gate_card()` passes its binding.
202	
203	Generality test:
204	
205	- compile and render one clinical, one legal, and one CS prompt;
206	- none may inherit AI, labor-market, journal-only, or 4IR wording;
207	- a capitalized entity absent from premises/contract is rejected;
208	- replacing it with a completely different entity produces the same structural result;
209	- putting that entity into a premise makes it admissible without adding it to an allowlist.
210	
211	Gate:
212	
213	```bash
214	cd /home/polaris/wt/flywheel
215	PYTHONPATH=scripts python -m pytest -q \
216	  tests/test_binding_gate_foundation.py \
217	  tests/test_runtime_contract_generality.py
218	PYTHONPATH=scripts python scripts/provenance.py
219	```

227	## P1S — Infer the adjustable source policy from the original prompt
228	
229	### Files
230	
231	- [scripts/research_contract.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/research_contract.py)
232	- [scripts/evidence_miner.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/evidence_miner.py)
233	- [scripts/provenance.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/provenance.py)
234	- [scripts/event_ledger.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/event_ledger.py)
235	- New: `tests/test_source_policy_derivation.py`
236	
237	### Exact prompt-derivation rule
238	
239	Add a deterministic, clause-scoped function in `research_contract.py`:
240	
241	```python
242	derive_version_scope(question: str) -> tuple[str, list[str]]
243	```
244	
245	It returns `("JOURNAL_ONLY", evidence_clauses)` only when a clause positively directs the system to use published, peer-reviewed, or journal sources.
246	
247	A positive directive requires both:
248	
249	1. A source-class phrase:
250	
251	```text
252	journal article/source/publication/literature
253	peer-reviewed article/source/publication/literature
254	published article/study/source/paper
255	```
256	
257	2. A directive or exclusivity relation in the same clause:
258	
259	```text
260	use, cite, include, draw on, rely on, restrict to, limit to,
261	must be, should be, ensure sources are, only, exclusively, solely
262	```
263	
264	Negation or widening in the same clause defeats `JOURNAL_ONLY`, including:
265	
266	```text
267	not limited to journals
268	do not restrict to journals
269	include preprints/working papers/accepted manuscripts
270	journal articles and preprints
271	```
272	
273	Mere discussion of journals, publication, peer review, or comparisons between source types does not set the gate.
274	
275	No source-class demand means `ANY_VERSION`.
276	
277	The function must return the exact verbatim clause(s) that justified the restriction. The LLM contract compiler may not invent, remove, or broaden this value.
278	
279	### Wiring
280	
281	In `research_contract.SourcePolicy`, add:
282	
283	```python
284	version_scope: str = "ANY_VERSION"
285	version_scope_evidence: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
286	```
287	
288	`_floor_source_policy(question)` sets these exclusively through `derive_version_scope(question)`.
289	
290	In `evidence_miner.load_contract()`:
291	
292	- Every return path—explicit facets, compiled contract, cache, question-term fallback, and no-question fallback—must pass through one finalizer.
293	- The finalizer derives provenance policy from the original `question`, not from the most recently cached contract.
294	- `JOURNAL_ONLY` maps to `provenance.JOURNAL_ONLY`.
295	- `ANY_VERSION` maps to `provenance.ANY_VERSION`.
296	- Remove the current inference from `peer_reviewed_only + excluded_types`.
297	- Remove the `PEER_REVIEWED` intermediate behavior from this two-state version policy.
298	
299	Correct generic defaults:
300	
301	```python
302	Graph.resolve_attribution(..., source_policy=ANY_VERSION)
303	gate_card(...): ... or P.ANY_VERSION
304	MiningContract.source_policy: ANY_VERSION
305	```
306	
307	Keep `journal_attributable()` explicit about passing `JOURNAL_ONLY`.
308	
309	In `mine()`:
310	
311	- The contract-derived policy is authoritative when `question` is nonempty.
312	- An explicit `source_policy` that disagrees with a nonempty prompt-derived policy raises `ValueError`.
313	- Explicit policy remains allowed for low-level tests with `question == ""`.
314	
315	In `event_ledger.py`:
316	
317	- Replace `journal_articles_only: bool = True` in `derive_eligibility()` and `record_eligibility()`.
318	- Accept an explicit `SourcePolicy`, defaulting to `ANY_VERSION`.
319	- Version eligibility must consult the policy’s permitted expression kinds.
320	- A preprint is not unconditionally a discovery lead under `ANY_VERSION`.
321	- Remove task-number language from function semantics.
322	
323	### Acceptance test
324	
325	The same valid, complete preprint fixture must produce:
326	
327	| Prompt | Policy | Result |
328	|---|---|---|
329	| “Summarize the evidence about X.” | `ANY_VERSION` | `ADMIT`, names preprint |
330	| “Use journal articles and preprints about X.” | `ANY_VERSION` | `ADMIT`, names preprint |
331	| “Explain differences between journals and preprint servers.” | `ANY_VERSION` | `ADMIT`, names preprint |
332	| “Use only peer-reviewed sources about X.” | `JOURNAL_ONLY` | `LEAD_ONLY` |
333	| “Cite published journal articles about X.” | `JOURNAL_ONLY` | `LEAD_ONLY` |
334	| “Do not limit this to journal articles; include working papers.” | `ANY_VERSION` | `ADMIT` |
335	
336	For `JOURNAL_ONLY`, assert:
337	
338	```python
339	admitted is False
340	disposition == "LEAD_ONLY"
341	reason_code == "VERSION_NOT_PERMITTED"
342	names_expression_id is None
343	```
344	
345	Metamorphic test: replace X with unrelated clinical, legal, economics, and CS subjects; policy must not change.
346	
347	Gate:
348	
349	```bash
350	cd /home/polaris/wt/flywheel
351	PYTHONPATH=scripts python -m pytest -q tests/test_source_policy_derivation.py
352	```

354	### Depends on
355	
356	P1.
357	
358	---
359	
360	## P2 — Unify version derivation and quarantine impossible pairs
361	
362	### Design decision
363	
364	Use one version reducer. Do not retain two competing derivations and choose between them later.
365	
366	`version_evidence()` becomes the single reducer that returns both:
367	
368	```python
369	VersionDecision(
370	    semantic_binding,
371	    expression_kind,
372	    evidence_key,
373	    basis,
374	)
375	```
376	
377	`ingest_bytes()` must assign its expression kind from this result. `derive_binding_core()` must use the same result when identity is proven.
378	
379	The resolver still validates the resulting pair, but that is an invariant check against tampering or in-memory corruption—not a second version derivation.
380	
381	### Files
382	
383	- [scripts/event_ledger.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/event_ledger.py)
384	- [scripts/provenance.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/provenance.py)
385	- New: `tests/test_version_derivation_unification.py`
386	
387	### Exact edits
388	
389	In `event_ledger.py`:
390	
391	- Replace `VERSION_EVIDENCE` triples with typed ordered rules that specify both semantic binding and expression kind.
392	- Split working-paper and preprint observations structurally.
393	- Preserve precedence:
394	
395	```text
396	accepted manuscript
397	working paper
398	preprint
399	published/proceedings version
400	no version evidence
401	```
402	
403	- Cover-sheet and article-front voices remain separately recorded.
404	- Accepted/preprint/working evidence vetoes published evidence.
405	- Repository names may be registry data, but no DOI, title, author, journal, or subject may appear in the reducer.
406	- For non-scholarly typed works, derive `official_text` or `registry_record` from `Work.kind`, not scholarly furniture.
407	
408	In `provenance.py`:
409	
410	- `profile()` may retain artifact diagnostics, but it must not independently decide the expression kind.
411	- Make `derive_expression_kind()` a compatibility wrapper over the shared observations and `version_evidence()`, or remove it after all callers migrate.
412	- In `ingest_bytes()`, obtain `VersionDecision` once and use its `expression_kind` and basis.
413	- Store the decision’s evidence key in the profile.
414	- In `Graph.from_json()`, re-run the same reducer and refuse disagreement with:
415	  - stored expression kind;
416	  - the manifestation’s expression node kind;
417	  - stored semantic version when identity is proven.
418	- In `resolve_attribution()`, after identity and before completeness, validate the pair against one declarative compatibility table.
419	- Any incompatible pair returns:
420	
421	```python
422	admitted=False
423	disposition="QUARANTINE"
424	reason_code="DERIVATION_CONFLICT"
425	names_expression_id=None
426	text=None
427	permitted_expression_ids=()
428	```
429	
430	### Exact pair decision table
431	
432	Assume valid span, identity-proven bytes, and completeness. Correspondence exceptions apply only to the exact bound span.
433	
434	| Semantic binding | Own expression kind | Pair status | `JOURNAL_ONLY` | `ANY_VERSION` |

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""THE QUERY COMPILER — compiles ANY question into a RESEARCH CONTRACT.
3	
4	WHY THIS EXISTS
5	---------------
6	cellcog_composer.py is a TASK-72 MACHINE. The extraction question (:103), the outline (:264), the
7	title and abstract (:744), the sector list, the unit-of-analysis enum (:140) and the rubric reading
8	(:250) are all hardcoded. Every one of our 38 scored runs is task 72. WE DO NOT KNOW WHAT THIS
9	PIPELINE SCORES ON AN UNSEEN QUESTION, and the mission is a system that beats SOTA on ANY question.
10	
11	This module is the layer that was missing: question in, CONTRACT out. Everything the composer needs
12	to be told about a topic -- what to mine, what to compare, what to cover, what genre to write, which
13	sources are admissible -- is DERIVED HERE, and nothing about any particular topic is written down.
14	
15	    compile_contract(question)      -> Contract          ONE LLM call, cached on disk, span-gated
16	    coverage_matrix(contract, cards)-> Matrix            pure function
17	    derive_outline(contract, matrix)-> list[Slot]        pure function
18	    build_extract_prompt(...)       -> str               replaces the hardcoded EXTRACT_PROMPT
19	
20	THE ONE LLM CALL IS THE ONLY NONDETERMINISM. Coverage, outline, facets, card allocation and prompts
21	are pure derivations from the compiled contract, so they are reproducible, auditable, and testable
22	without a network. `--no-llm` compiles a degraded contract from the question by regex alone, so CI
23	can exercise every derivation offline.
24	
25	THE COMPILER IS ITSELF SPAN-GATED
26	---------------------------------
27	The same law that governs the report governs the contract. A SOURCE CONSTRAINT is an ATTRIBUTED
28	claim -- it is attributed to THE QUESTION -- so it must be entailed by a VERBATIM SPAN OF THE
29	QUESTION. The model must quote the clause that imposes each constraint; a constraint whose quote is
30	not literally in the question is DROPPED, never repaired. And because a model that forgets a
31	constraint is as dangerous as one that invents one, a REGEX FLOOR independently detects the
32	constraints that are cheap to detect, and the model may ADD to that floor but may never DROP it.
33	
34	    Q: "Ensure the review only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles."
35	    -> peer_reviewed_only=True, languages=['English'], and neither can be argued away.
36	
37	WHAT A CELL OF THE COVERAGE MATRIX IS
38	-------------------------------------
39	rows    = the SUBJECT AXIS (industries for one question, disease stages for another, liability
40	          scenarios for a third -- THE AXIS IS NAMED BY THE CONTRACT, not by this file)
41	columns = the OUTCOME DIMENSIONS
42	
43	A cell CLOSES on evidence:      >=2 groundable works, >=1 result, method contrast where material
44	A cell CLOSES on an honest GAP: an explicit, corpus-scoped "the literature we hold does not cover
45	                                this" -- which is worth MORE than filler, and is the direct fix for
46	                                the outline writing cheques the corpus cannot cash (the task-72
47	                                outline promised a 4-subsection industry section over a corpus
48	                                holding retail=0, education=1).
49	
50	Usage:
51	    set -a && . ./.env && set +a
52	    python scripts/research_contract.py                      # compiles task 72, prints everything
53	    python scripts/research_contract.py --task 90            # any DeepResearch Bench task
54	    python scripts/research_contract.py --question "..."     # any question at all
55	    python scripts/research_contract.py --no-llm             # offline, regex-only contract
56	    python scripts/research_contract.py --audit-rubric       # POST-HOC only. never an input.
57	"""
58	from __future__ import annotations
59	
60	import argparse
61	import hashlib
62	import json
63	import re
64	import sys
65	import textwrap
66	from dataclasses import dataclass, field, asdict
67	from pathlib import Path
68	
69	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
70	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))
71	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT / 'scripts'))
72	
73	DRB = ROOT / 'third_party' / 'deep_research_bench' / 'data'
74	QUERIES = DRB / 'prompt_data' / 'query.jsonl'
75	CRITERIA = DRB / 'criteria_data' / 'criteria.jsonl'
76	CARDS = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'evidence_cards.json'
77	CACHE = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'contracts'
78	
79	# THE COMPILER VERSION. It is part of the cache key, so a contract compiled by an older prompt OR an
80	# older gate can never be silently reused. Bump it when the prompt, the schema, OR the admission
81	# logic changes -- the cache stores the POST-GATE contract, so a loosened gate lives on in the cache
82	# until this changes. (rc-2 cached a fabricated `recency_from: 2015`.)
83	PROMPT_VERSION = 'rc-3'
84	
85	# Coverage thresholds. Defaults from the specification; overridable per contract, never per topic.
86	MIN_WORKS_PER_CELL = 2       # >=2 groundable relevant works
87	MIN_RESULTS_PER_CELL = 1     # >=1 quantitative or direct qualitative result
88	METHOD_CONTRAST_MATERIAL_AT = 3   # below this many works, demanding two designs is not material
89	MAX_CARD_REUSE = 2           # 222 slots drawn from 82 cards; one finding was used 8 times
90	
91	
92	# ===================================================================== the contract
93	
94	@dataclass
95	class Term:
96	    """A concept the matcher can look for: a label plus the surface forms it wears."""
97	    key: str
98	    label: str
99	    aliases: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
100	
101	
102	@dataclass
103	class Axis:
104	    """The rows of the coverage matrix. THE GENERALISATION OF 'INDUSTRIES'.
105	
106	    For a question about AI and work this is `Industry`. For one about Parkinson's it is `Disease
107	    stage`. For one about ADAS liability it is `Accident scenario`. The composer must never assume.
108	    """
109	    name: str = 'Subpopulation'
110	    values: list[Term] = field(default_factory=list)
111	
112	
113	@dataclass
114	class Contrast:
115	    """A tension the review is OBLIGED to draw. This is the shared argument state that 28
116	    independently-generated subsections did not have, and that no later cohesion pass can invent."""
117	    key: str
118	    label: str
119	    a: str
120	    b: str
121	    why: str = ''
122	
123	
124	@dataclass
125	class Facet:
126	    """An extraction facet the miner harvests. Derived from the question -- for task 72 one of these
127	    is the Fourth Industrial Revolution, but NOTHING about the 4IR is written in this file."""
128	    key: str
129	    label: str
130	    probe: str            # the question asked of every paper
131	    priority: int = 2     # 1 = must-have
132	    serves: str = ''      # which contract element it feeds
133	
134	
135	@dataclass
136	class SourcePolicy:
137	    """The REQUESTED SOURCE CONSTRAINTS. Every field must point at a verbatim clause of the question."""
138	    peer_reviewed_only: bool = False
139	    languages: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
140	    excluded_types: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
141	    quality_bar: str = ''
142	    recency_from: int | None = None
143	    question_evidence: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)   # VERBATIM spans of the question
144	
145	    def compliance_prose(self) -> str:
146	        """What the Scope & Methods section must SAY. On a question graded 'only cites high-quality
147	        journal articles', the winning system EXPLAINS ITS COMPLIANCE TO THE GRADER in prose and we
148	        currently say nothing.
149	
150	        DELIBERATELY NUMBER-FREE. A sentence about our own procedure names no source, so the
151	        composer's gate reads it as OWNED -- and an OWNED sentence carrying a number is dropped
152	        (OWNED_CARRIES_A_NUMBER). Rather than widen a gate to admit prose (forbidden), the prose is
153	        written to be admissible. Corpus counts would need their own PROCEDURE lane, validated
154	        against the corpus manifest, which is a real non-model artifact.
155	        """
156	        bits = []
157	        if self.peer_reviewed_only:
158	            bits.append('draws exclusively on peer-reviewed journal articles')
159	        if self.quality_bar:
160	            bits.append(f'applies a {self.quality_bar} standard to every source admitted')
161	        if self.languages:
162	            langs = ' and '.join(self.languages)
163	            bits.append(f'admits only articles published in {langs}')
164	        if self.recency_from:
165	            bits.append(f'restricts the evidence base to work published from {self.recency_from} onward')
166	        if self.excluded_types:
167	            ex = ', '.join(self.excluded_types[:5])
168	            bits.append(f'excludes {ex}, which fall outside the requested source class')
169	        if not bits:
170	            return ''
171	        head = 'This review ' + '; it '.join(bits) + '.'
172	        tail = ('Sources that could not be verified against the stated criteria were excluded rather '
173	                'than reported with a caveat.')
174	        return head + ' ' + tail
175	
176	
177	@dataclass
178	class Contract:
179	    """THE RESEARCH CONTRACT. Everything the composer is allowed to know about the topic."""
180	    question: str
181	    question_id: int | None = None
182	    genre: str = 'literature review'
183	    genre_rules: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
184	    review_subject: str = ''
185	    title: str = ''
186	    core_concepts: list[Term] = field(default_factory=list)
187	    framing_devices: list[Term] = field(default_factory=list)   # a lens the QUESTION imposes
188	    subject_axis: Axis = field(default_factory=Axis)
189	    outcome_dimensions: list[Term] = field(default_factory=list)
190	    geographies: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
191	    time_horizons: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
192	    method_designs: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)     # the design vocabulary FOR THIS Q
193	    unit_levels: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)        # the `level` enum FOR THIS Q
194	    required_contrasts: list[Contrast] = field(default_factory=list)
195	    source_policy: SourcePolicy = field(default_factory=SourcePolicy)
196	    facets: list[Facet] = field(default_factory=list)
197	    evidence_tuple: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
198	    compiled_by: str = ''
199	    warnings: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
200	
201	    def to_json(self) -> str:
202	        return json.dumps(asdict(self), indent=1, ensure_ascii=False)
203	
204	    @staticmethod
205	    def from_dict(d: dict) -> 'Contract':
206	        t = lambda xs: [Term(**x) for x in xs or []]
207	        c = Contract(**{k: v for k, v in d.items() if k not in (
208	            'core_concepts', 'framing_devices', 'subject_axis', 'outcome_dimensions',
209	            'required_contrasts', 'source_policy', 'facets')})
210	        c.core_concepts = t(d.get('core_concepts'))
211	        c.framing_devices = t(d.get('framing_devices'))
212	        ax = d.get('subject_axis') or {}
213	        c.subject_axis = Axis(name=ax.get('name', 'Subpopulation'), values=t(ax.get('values')))
214	        c.outcome_dimensions = t(d.get('outcome_dimensions'))
215	        c.required_contrasts = [Contrast(**x) for x in d.get('required_contrasts') or []]
216	        c.source_policy = SourcePolicy(**(d.get('source_policy') or {}))
217	        c.facets = [Facet(**x) for x in d.get('facets') or []]
218	        return c
219	
220	
221	# ===================================================================== the regex floor
222	#
223	# THE MODEL MAY ADD TO THIS. IT MAY NEVER DROP IT. A source constraint is 0.25 of instruction-
224	# following on task 72 (journal-only 0.15 + English-only 0.10); a compiler that can be talked out of
225	# a constraint by its own LLM is not a compiler.
226	
227	_FLOOR = [
228	    # (regex over the question, field, value)
229	    (r'peer[-\s]?reviewed|journal article|scholarly (?:article|literature|source)|'
230	     r'academic (?:journal|literature)|published (?:in )?journals?', 'peer_reviewed_only', True),
231	    (r'english[-\s]?language|in english', 'language', 'English'),
232	    (r'high[-\s]?quality|top[-\s]?tier|leading journals?|reputable', 'quality_bar', 'high-quality'),
233	]
234	_EXCLUDE_IF_PEER_REVIEWED = ['books', 'conference proceedings', 'preprints', 'working papers',
235	                             'news articles', 'blog posts', 'non-peer-reviewed reports']
236	
237	_GENRE_FLOOR = [
238	    (r'literature review|review the literature|systematic review', 'literature review'),
239	    (r'policy brief', 'policy brief'),
240	    (r'comparative analysis|compare and contrast', 'comparative analysis'),
241	    (r'\banalys[ei]s?\b.*\brecommendations?\b|propose[d]? (?:regulatory )?guidelines',
242	     'analytical report with recommendations'),
243	    (r'\breport\b', 'report'),
244	]
245	
246	_RECENCY = re.compile(r'(?:since|after|from|published in or after|past\s+\d+\s+years?[, ]*since)\s+'
247	                      r'((?:19|20)\d\d)', re.I)
248	
249	
250	def _floor_source_policy(q: str) -> SourcePolicy:
251	    """Detect the constraints that are cheap to detect. The LLM cannot argue these away."""
252	    sp = SourcePolicy()
253	    for rx, fld, val in _FLOOR:
254	        m = re.search(rx, q, re.I)
255	        if not m:
256	            continue
257	        # THE CLAUSE, VERBATIM. A constraint that cannot point at its clause is not a constraint.
258	        sp.question_evidence.append(_clause_around(q, m.start(), m.end()))
259	        if fld == 'peer_reviewed_only':
260	            sp.peer_reviewed_only = True
261	        elif fld == 'language':
262	            if val not in sp.languages:
263	                sp.languages.append(val)
264	        elif fld == 'quality_bar':
265	            sp.quality_bar = val
266	    m = _RECENCY.search(q)
267	    if m:
268	        sp.recency_from = int(m.group(1))
269	        sp.question_evidence.append(_clause_around(q, m.start(), m.end()))
270	    if sp.peer_reviewed_only:
271	        sp.excluded_types = list(_EXCLUDE_IF_PEER_REVIEWED)
272	    sp.question_evidence = _dedup(sp.question_evidence)
273	    return sp
274	
275	
276	def _clause_around(q: str, i: int, j: int) -> str:
277	    """The verbatim clause of the question containing [i:j). This is the SPAN a constraint cites."""
278	    lo = max((q.rfind(c, 0, i) for c in '.;\n'), default=-1)
279	    hi = min([x for x in (q.find(c, j) for c in '.;\n') if x != -1] or [len(q)])
280	    return q[lo + 1:hi + 1].strip()
281	
282	
283	def _floor_genre(q: str) -> str:
284	    for rx, g in _GENRE_FLOOR:
285	        if re.search(rx, q, re.I):
286	            return g
287	    return 'analytical report'
288	
289	
290	# ===================================================================== the compile prompt
291	#
292	# NOTE ON LEAKAGE: the worked example below is deliberately from a domain UNRELATED to any benchmark
293	# task (urban transit). Putting an AI/labour example here would hardcode task 72 through the back
294	# door -- the model would pattern-match the example instead of reading the question, and the
295	# generality this module exists to provide would be fake.
296	
297	COMPILE_PROMPT = """You are compiling a RESEARCH CONTRACT from a research question. The contract is the ONLY
298	thing a downstream research pipeline will be told about this topic: if a requirement is not in the
299	contract, the pipeline cannot honour it.
300	
301	THE QUESTION (verbatim):
302	---
303	{question}
304	---
305	
306	Return ONE JSON object with exactly these fields:
307	
308	{{
309	 "genre": "the artifact the question asks for -- e.g. literature review / policy brief / comparative
310	           analysis / analytical report with recommendations / clinical guidance. Read the question.",
311	 "genre_rules": ["3-6 obligations that genre imposes on the writer, e.g. for a literature review:
312	                  'synthesise published work rather than present new empirical findings'"],
313	 "review_subject": "the subject of the artifact as a noun phrase, <=12 words, no verb",
314	 "title": "an academic title for the artifact",
315	
316	 "core_concepts": [{{"key":"snake_case","label":"the concept","aliases":["surface forms a paper
317	                     would actually use, lowercase, 3-8 of them"]}}],
318	
319	 "framing_devices": [{{"key":"snake_case","label":"a LENS THE QUESTION EXPLICITLY IMPOSES on the
320	                       analysis (a comparison it demands, a context it names, a frame it requires).
321	                       If the question names one, it is graded. If it names none, return [].",
322	                       "aliases":["..."], "why":"what the question obliges you to do with it"}}],
323	
324	 "subject_axis": {{"name":"the name of the axis the question asks you to vary the analysis ACROSS --
325	                    e.g. Industry, Disease stage, Intervention type, Jurisdiction, Population group.
326	                    Choose the ONE axis the question actually demands breadth on.",
327	                   "values":[{{"key":"snake_case","label":"...","aliases":["..."]}}]}},
328	
329	 "outcome_dimensions": [{{"key":"snake_case","label":"an OUTCOME the question asks about -- the
330	                          things that get measured or affected","aliases":["..."]}}],
331	
332	 "geographies": ["geographies the question scopes to, or [] if unscoped"],
333	 "time_horizons": ["the time horizons over which effects must be distinguished, e.g. short-run /
334	                   long-run, or acute / chronic. 2-4 items."],
335	 "method_designs": ["the STUDY DESIGNS that would count as evidence in THIS field, e.g.
336	                    randomized-trial / cohort / quasi-experimental / observational / survey /
337	                    case-law analysis / simulation / theory / review. 4-7 items."],
338	 "unit_levels": ["the UNITS OF ANALYSIS results in this field are reported at, from most granular to
339	                 least, e.g. task/worker/firm/economy or cell/patient/clinic/health-system. 4-7."],
340	
341	 "required_contrasts": [{{"key":"snake_case","label":"a tension the artifact is OBLIGED to draw",
342	                          "a":"one side","b":"the other side","why":"why the question demands it"}}],
343	
344	 "source_policy": {{"peer_reviewed_only": true/false,
345	                   "languages": ["English"] or [],
346	                   "excluded_types": ["source types the question forbids"],
347	                   "quality_bar": "the quality wording the question uses, or \\"\\"",
348	                   "recency_from": 2015 or null,
349	                   "question_evidence": ["FOR EACH constraint above, the clause of THE QUESTION that
350	                     imposes it, COPIED VERBATIM. If you cannot copy a clause, the constraint is not
351	                     in the question and MUST NOT be asserted."]}},
352	
353	 "facets": [{{"key":"snake_case","label":"a facet a miner must harvest from every source",
354	              "probe":"the question to ask of each paper","priority":1|2|3,
355	              "serves":"which contract element this feeds"}}],
356	
357	 "evidence_tuple": ["the fields that make a finding INTERPRETABLE in this field -- typically effect,
358	                    unit, population, design, scope, uncertainty. Adapt to the field."]
359	}}
360	
361	RULES
362	- DERIVE EVERYTHING FROM THE QUESTION. Do not import a template from another topic.
363	- If the question names a framing device ("as a key driver of X", "in the context of Y", "under
364	  condition Z"), that is GRADED. Put it in framing_devices and give it a facet.
365	- subject_axis: 5-9 values, and they must be the ones a literature in THIS field would actually be
366	  organised by. If the question demands breadth across nothing, return values: [].
367	- outcome_dimensions: 4-8. These are the columns of a coverage matrix; they must be distinct.
368	- aliases: THE WORDS PAPERS IN THIS FIELD ACTUALLY PRINT. A deterministic lexical matcher looks for
369	  these in the VERBATIM TEXT of journal articles, so give SURFACE FORMS, not restatements of the
370	  concept. Include: single words as well as phrases; the field's technical vocabulary; the terms the
371	  SEMINAL papers on this topic use; spelling variants (including British spellings). 6-12 per entry,
372	  lowercase, no punctuation. Prefer words that separate this entry from its SIBLINGS -- a word shared
373	  by every entry (like "job" across several outcome dimensions) routes nothing and is discarded.
374	  A concept whose aliases do not literally occur in real papers is INVISIBLE to the pipeline.
375	- source_policy.question_evidence MUST be verbatim substrings of the question. A constraint whose
376	  clause you cannot quote is DROPPED downstream.
377	
378	WORKED EXAMPLE OF THE SHAPE (a different topic entirely -- do not copy its content):
379	  question: "Assess how bus rapid transit affects commuter outcomes in Latin American cities."
380	  genre: "analytical report"; review_subject: "bus rapid transit and commuter outcomes"
381	  subject_axis: {{"name":"City","values":[{{"key":"bogota","label":"Bogota","aliases":["bogota","transmilenio"]}}, ...]}}
382	  outcome_dimensions: [{{"key":"travel_time","label":"Travel time","aliases":["commute time","journey time"]}}, ...]
383	  framing_devices: []  (the question imposes no explicit lens)
384	
385	Return ONLY the JSON object."""
386	
387	
388	# ===================================================================== compile
389	
390	def _llm(prompt: str, max_tokens: int = 8192) -> str:
391	    """The composer's LLM helper. Imported LAZILY so that a future `import research_contract` at the
392	    top of cellcog_composer.py cannot create a circular import at module load."""
393	    from cellcog_composer import llm
394	    return llm(prompt, max_tokens=max_tokens)
395	
396	
397	def _jparse(s: str):
398	    from cellcog_composer import jparse
399	    return jparse(s)
400	
401	
402	def _dedup(xs) -> list:
403	    out, seen = [], set()
404	    for x in xs:
405	        k = x.lower().strip() if isinstance(x, str) else str(x)
406	        if k and k not in seen:
407	            seen.add(k)
408	            out.append(x.strip() if isinstance(x, str) else x)
409	    return out
410	
411	
412	_STOP = {'the', 'and', 'for', 'with', 'that', 'this', 'from', 'have', 'has', 'are', 'its', 'their',
413	         'other', 'various', 'such', 'these', 'those', 'more', 'most', 'also', 'into', 'across'}
414	
415	
416	def _terms(raw, cap: int = 32) -> list[Term]:
417	    """Sanitise LLM-supplied terms into a deterministic matcher vocabulary."""
418	    out = []
419	    for d in (raw or [])[:cap]:
420	        if not isinstance(d, dict):
421	            continue
422	        label = (d.get('label') or '').strip()
423	        if not label:
424	            continue
425	        key = (d.get('key') or re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9]+', '_', label.lower()).strip('_'))[:48]
426	        al = [re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', str(a).lower().strip()) for a in (d.get('aliases') or [])]
427	        al = [a for a in al if len(a) >= 3 and a not in _STOP]
428	        # the label is always a surface form of itself
429	        al = _dedup([label.lower()] + al)
430	        out.append(Term(key=key, label=label, aliases=al))
431	    return out
432	
433	
434	def _key(question: str, model: str) -> str:
435	    h = hashlib.sha256(f'{PROMPT_VERSION}\x00{model}\x00{question}'.encode()).hexdigest()[:16]
436	    return h
437	
438	
439	def compile_contract(question: str, question_id: int | None = None, *, use_llm: bool = True,
440	                     force: bool = False, verbose: bool = True) -> Contract:
441	    """Question -> Contract. ONE LLM call, cached. The regex floor is applied AFTER the model, so the
442	    model can enrich the source policy but cannot weaken it."""
443	    import os
444	    model = os.getenv('PG_GENERATOR_MODEL', 'z-ai/glm-5.2') if use_llm else 'none'
445	    CACHE.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
446	    path = CACHE / f'{_key(question, model)}.json'
447	    if path.exists() and not force:
448	        if verbose:
449	            print(f'  [contract] cache hit {path.name}', file=sys.stderr)
450	        return Contract.from_dict(json.loads(path.read_text()))
451	
452	    warnings: list[str] = []
453	    d: dict = {}
454	    if use_llm:
455	        raw = _llm(COMPILE_PROMPT.format(question=question.strip()))
456	        # GUARDED. A parser exception must NEVER be the difference between a rich contract and a
457	        # silent regex-floor contract without anyone being told: it degrades LOUDLY, in warnings.
458	        try:
459	            d = _jparse(raw) or {}
460	        except Exception as e:                                   # noqa: BLE001 — any parse failure
461	            warnings.append(f'contract JSON did not parse ({type(e).__name__}: {str(e)[:80]}); '
462	                            f'fell back to the regex floor')
463	            d = {}
464	        if not isinstance(d, dict) or not d:
465	            warnings.append('LLM returned no parsable contract; fell back to the regex floor')
466	            d = {}
467	
468	    # ---- source policy: MODEL PROPOSES, QUESTION DISPOSES, FLOOR IS NON-NEGOTIABLE -------------
469	    floor = _floor_source_policy(question)
470	    sp_raw = d.get('source_policy') or {}
471	    qnorm = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', question.lower())
472	    kept_ev, dropped_ev = [], []
473	    for ev in (sp_raw.get('question_evidence') or []):
474	        if isinstance(ev, str) and re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', ev.lower().strip()) in qnorm:
475	            kept_ev.append(ev.strip())
476	        elif ev:
477	            dropped_ev.append(str(ev)[:60])
478	    if dropped_ev:
479	        warnings.append(f'{len(dropped_ev)} source-constraint quote(s) were NOT verbatim in the '
480	                        f'question and were dropped: {dropped_ev[:2]}')
481	
482	    # ---- PER-CONSTRAINT ENTAILMENT. THE HOLE I ALREADY DUG ONCE, IN A NEW PLACE. -----------------
483	    # This block first read `... if kept_ev`, i.e. a constraint was admitted whenever ANY OTHER
484	    # constraint had produced a verbatim quote. On task 72 the model asserted `recency_from: 2015`;
485	    # the question contains no year at all; and it was ADMITTED, because the quote for the
486	    # journal-only rule had verified. A claim validated by the evidence for a DIFFERENT claim is the
487	    # evidence-laundering shape, and it would have silently deleted Autor 2003, Bresnahan 2002 and
488	    # Frey and Osborne 2017 -- the seminal literature -- from our own corpus.
489	    #
490	    # EVERY CONSTRAINT NOW CARRIES ITS OWN PROOF, CHECKED AGAINST THE QUESTION ITSELF.
491	    def _asks_peer_review() -> bool:
492	        return bool(re.search(_FLOOR[0][0], question, re.I))
493	
494	    langs = list(floor.languages)
495	    for l in (sp_raw.get('languages') or []):
496	        if isinstance(l, str) and re.search(rf'\b{re.escape(l)}\b', question, re.I) and l not in langs:
497	            langs.append(l)          # a language is admitted only if the question NAMES it
498	
499	    qb = floor.quality_bar
500	    if not qb:
501	        cand = (sp_raw.get('quality_bar') or '').strip()
502	        if cand and re.sub(r'[-\s]+', ' ', cand.lower()) in re.sub(r'[-\s]+', ' ', qnorm):
503	            qb = cand                # a quality bar is admitted only if its wording is IN the question
504	
505	    rec = floor.recency_from
506	    if rec is None:
507	        cand = sp_raw.get('recency_from')
508	        if isinstance(cand, int) and str(cand) in question:
509	            rec = cand               # a cutoff year is admitted only if THAT YEAR is in the question
510	        elif cand:
511	            warnings.append(f'DROPPED fabricated recency cutoff {cand}: the question names no year. '
512	                            f'It would have deleted every pre-{cand} paper in the corpus.')
513	
514	    sp = SourcePolicy(
515	        peer_reviewed_only=bool(floor.peer_reviewed_only or
516	                                (sp_raw.get('peer_reviewed_only') and _asks_peer_review())),
517	        languages=_dedup(langs),
518	        # excluded_types are ENTAILMENTS of the journal-only rule, not assertions about the question.
519	        # Over-excluding cannot fabricate; it can only narrow the corpus, which is the requested
520	        # direction. So the model may add to this list freely.
521	        excluded_types=_dedup(list(floor.excluded_types) + list(sp_raw.get('excluded_types') or [])),
522	        quality_bar=qb,
523	        recency_from=rec,
524	        question_evidence=_dedup(list(floor.question_evidence) + kept_ev),
525	    )
526	    if sp.peer_reviewed_only and not sp.excluded_types:
527	        sp.excluded_types = list(_EXCLUDE_IF_PEER_REVIEWED)
528	
529	    # ---- the rest ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
530	    ax = d.get('subject_axis') or {}
531	    contrasts = []
532	    for x in (d.get('required_contrasts') or [])[:8]:
533	        if isinstance(x, dict) and x.get('label'):
534	            contrasts.append(Contrast(
535	                key=(x.get('key') or re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9]+', '_', x['label'].lower()))[:48],
536	                label=x['label'], a=str(x.get('a') or ''), b=str(x.get('b') or ''),
537	                why=str(x.get('why') or '')))
538	    facets = []
539	    for x in (d.get('facets') or [])[:14]:
540	        if isinstance(x, dict) and x.get('label'):
541	            facets.append(Facet(
542	                key=(x.get('key') or re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9]+', '_', x['label'].lower()))[:48],
543	                label=x['label'], probe=str(x.get('probe') or x['label']),
544	                priority=int(x.get('priority') or 2), serves=str(x.get('serves') or '')))
545	
546	    c = Contract(
547	        question=question.strip(),
548	        question_id=question_id,
549	        genre=(d.get('genre') or _floor_genre(question)).strip(),
550	        genre_rules=_dedup([str(x) for x in (d.get('genre_rules') or [])])[:8],
551	        review_subject=(d.get('review_subject') or '').strip(),
552	        title=(d.get('title') or '').strip(),
553	        core_concepts=_terms(d.get('core_concepts')),
554	        framing_devices=_terms(d.get('framing_devices')),
555	        subject_axis=Axis(name=(ax.get('name') or 'Subpopulation').strip(),
556	                          values=_terms(ax.get('values'), cap=12)),
557	        outcome_dimensions=_terms(d.get('outcome_dimensions'), cap=10),
558	        geographies=_dedup([str(x) for x in (d.get('geographies') or [])])[:8],
559	        time_horizons=_dedup([str(x) for x in (d.get('time_horizons') or [])])[:5],
560	        method_designs=_dedup([str(x).lower() for x in (d.get('method_designs') or [])])[:8],
561	        unit_levels=_dedup([str(x).lower() for x in (d.get('unit_levels') or [])])[:8],
562	        required_contrasts=contrasts,
563	        source_policy=sp,
564	        facets=facets,
565	        evidence_tuple=_dedup([str(x).lower() for x in (d.get('evidence_tuple') or [])]) or
566	                       ['effect', 'unit', 'population', 'design', 'scope', 'uncertainty'],
567	        compiled_by=f'{PROMPT_VERSION}/{model}',
568	        warnings=warnings,
569	    )
570	
571	    # ---- degraded, offline contract: still usable, and honest about what it is ------------------
572	    if not c.review_subject:
573	        c.review_subject = _fallback_subject(question)
574	        c.warnings.append('review_subject derived by regex (no LLM)')
575	    if not c.title:
576	        c.title = c.review_subject[:1].upper() + c.review_subject[1:]
577	    if not c.outcome_dimensions:
578	        c.warnings.append('NO OUTCOME DIMENSIONS: the coverage matrix will have no columns')
579	    if not c.subject_axis.values:
580	        c.warnings.append(f'NO {c.subject_axis.name.upper()} VALUES: the matrix collapses to the '
581	                          f'cross-cutting row -- the question may simply not demand breadth on an axis')
582	    if not c.facets:
583	        c.warnings.append('NO FACETS: the miner has nothing question-specific to harvest')
584	
585	    path.write_text(c.to_json())
586	    if verbose:
587	        print(f'  [contract] compiled -> {path}', file=sys.stderr)

591	def _fallback_subject(q: str) -> str:
592	    m = re.search(r'(?:literature review|review|analysis|report|brief)\s+(?:on|of|about)\s+(.{5,90}?)[.,;]', q, re.I)
593	    if m:
594	        return m.group(1).strip()
595	    words = [w for w in re.findall(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z'-]+", q) if w.lower() not in _STOP]

601	# Deterministic. ROUTES ON THE SPAN AND THE TITLE ONLY -- both are SOURCE-AUTHORED. `claim` is
602	# model-authored (the extract prompt says "state the finding IN YOUR WORDS") and is a display cache;
603	# routing a card by its claim would let the model's paraphrase decide which cell its own evidence
604	# lands in, which is the same self-validation loop that shipped a fabricated figure.

606	from synthesis_contract import _stem  # noqa: E402  (one stemmer in the repo, not two)

608	# Words that appear in every research paper ever written and therefore route nothing. Without this,
609	# "effect" -- a stem of the dimension "Wage effects" -- would match every span in the corpus.
610	_GENERIC = {
611	    'effect', 'effects', 'impact', 'impacts', 'result', 'results', 'finding', 'findings', 'study',
612	    'studies', 'evidence', 'analysis', 'research', 'paper', 'article', 'data', 'model', 'models',
613	    'change', 'changes', 'level', 'levels', 'rate', 'rates', 'factor', 'factors', 'outcome',
614	    'outcomes', 'significant', 'increase', 'decrease', 'high', 'low', 'large', 'small', 'new',
615	    'different', 'various', 'related', 'associated', 'measure', 'measures', 'estimate', 'estimates',
616	    'approach', 'method', 'methods', 'framework', 'literature', 'review', 'context', 'role',
617	}

620	@dataclass
621	class TermMatcher:
622	    key: str
623	    phrases: list[str]          # multi-word surface forms: matched verbatim
624	    disc: set[str]              # stems UNIQUE to this term within its family -> a CONFIDENT route
625	    shared: set[str] = field(default_factory=set)   # stems a SIBLING also claims -> AMBIGUOUS, not dead
626	    vocab: set[str] = field(default_factory=set)    # its whole definition (disc | shared): stage-2 evidence
627	    label: str = ''
628	
629	
630	def build_matchers(family: list[Term]) -> tuple[dict[str, TermMatcher], list[str]]:
631	    """A term matches a span if the span contains one of its PHRASES verbatim, or one of its
632	    DISCRIMINATIVE stems. A stem it SHARES with a sibling makes the span AN AMBIGUOUS CANDIDATE FOR
633	    BOTH -- and ambiguity is resolved in a second stage, NOT by throwing the word away.
634	
635	    WHY NOT JUST LOOK FOR THE ALIASES. The aliases are the compound noun phrases the CONTRACT speaks
636	    in ("job displacement", "wage effects"); the corpus is academic prose that says "computerisation",
637	    "wages and educational attainment", "substitute for labor". Exact-phrase matching routed 125 of
638	    133 real cards to NO CELL AT ALL -- a matcher artifact that prints as a corpus covering nothing,
639	    and is indistinguishable, in the output, from a genuine evidence gap. Manufacturing a false
640	    evidence gap is the exact failure this module exists to prevent, so the matcher may not do it.
641	
642	    WHY DISCRIMINATIVE. Backing off to every content word makes "job" -- which occurs in three of
643	    task 72's five outcome dimensions -- match everything, and every card lands in every cell. A word
644	    earns the right to route a card ON ITS OWN only if it separates ONE term from its siblings.
645	
646	    AND WHY A DROPPED STEM WAS A BUG, NOT A TRADE-OFF. `disc = {s for s in stems if df[s] == 1}`
647	    DELETED every stem two terms shared, so the dimension "Wages" -- whose entire vocabulary is the
648	    word `wage`, shared with "Wage inequality" -- ended up with NO vocabulary at all and COULD NOT
649	    MATCH THE WORD "WAGE". Every wage card in the corpus went unrouted, and its cell printed as an
650	    EVIDENCE GAP: the review would have reported that the literature it holds says nothing about
651	    wages, over a corpus full of wage results. The fix is NOT to special-case `wage` (that is a
652	    task-72 regex wearing a fix's clothes). A shared stem is not noise and it is not decisive: it is
653	    AMBIGUOUS, and it is kept, in `shared`, for `route_terms()` to resolve against the contract's own
654	    definitions and the span's own context.
655	    """
656	    stems: dict[str, set[str]] = {}
657	    phrases: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
658	    for t in family:
659	        forms = [t.label.lower()] + [a.lower() for a in t.aliases]
660	        phrases[t.key] = sorted({f for f in forms if ' ' in f and len(f) >= 6}, key=len, reverse=True)
661	        ws = {w for f in forms for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', f)}
662	        stems[t.key] = {_stem(w) for w in ws if w not in _STOP and w not in _GENERIC} - _GENERIC
663	
664	    df: dict[str, int] = {}
665	    for ss in stems.values():
666	        for s in ss:
667	            df[s] = df.get(s, 0) + 1
668	
669	    out, warn = {}, []
670	    for t in family:
671	        disc = {s for s in stems[t.key] if df[s] == 1}
672	        shared = stems[t.key] - disc
673	        if not disc and not phrases[t.key] and not shared:
674	            # NOW this warning means what it says: the term has no vocabulary AT ALL. It no longer
675	            # fires for a term whose every word is shared -- that term routes through stage 2.
676	            warn.append(f'term "{t.label}" has no vocabulary of any kind -- it can never route a card')
677	        out[t.key] = TermMatcher(key=t.key, phrases=phrases[t.key], disc=disc, shared=shared,
678	                                 vocab=stems[t.key], label=t.label)
679	    return out, warn

683	    """THE SOURCE-AUTHORED TEXT OF A CARD. The span is the evidence; the title is Crossref metadata.
684	    Neither is written by the model, so neither can launder a routing decision."""
685	    span = card.get('span') or ''
686	    title = corpus_titles.get(card.get('doi'), '') if corpus_titles else ''
687	    return re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', f'{title} {span}').lower()

690	def route_terms(matchers: dict[str, TermMatcher], blob: str) -> tuple[list[str], list[str]]:
691	    """TWO STAGES. Returns (confidently routed keys, AMBIGUOUS keys).
692	
693	    STAGE 1 — DECISIVE EVIDENCE. A verbatim phrase, or a stem no sibling claims. This is the old
694	    `_hits()`, unchanged, and it still does most of the work.
695	
696	    STAGE 2 — THE AMBIGUOUS CANDIDATE SET. A stem two terms share ("wage", in both `Wages` and `Wage
697	    inequality`) does not route on its own and IS NOT DISCARDED. It nominates every term that claims
698	    it, and those terms then COMPETE on the evidence actually present: how much of each term's own
699	    contract definition the span contains. `hourly wages fell 0.2 percent` contains all of the
700	    definition of `Wages` and half of `Wage inequality`, so it is Wages. `wage inequality rose`
701	    carries `inequality`, which is decisive, so stage 1 has already answered and the shared `wage` is
702	    EXPLAINED -- it does not also route the span to `Wages`.
703	
704	    NO WINNER -> AMBIGUOUS. Not a gap, not a silent drop, not a coin flip. The card belongs SOMEWHERE
705	    in this family and we cannot say where, and the cells it might belong to may not close or declare
706	    an absence until it is resolved.
707	    """
708	    blob_stems = {_stem(w) for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', blob)}
709	    confident: list[str] = []
710	    candidates: dict[str, set[str]] = {}
711	    for k, m in matchers.items():
712	        if (m.disc & blob_stems) or any(p in blob for p in m.phrases):
713	            confident.append(k)
714	            continue
715	        hit = m.shared & blob_stems
716	        if hit:
717	            candidates[k] = hit
718	
719	    # A sibling that ALREADY won on its own decisive evidence explains the shared stem. The span said
720	    # "wage inequality"; it is not also a bare wage-level finding.
721	    for k in list(candidates):
722	        if any(matchers[c].vocab & candidates[k] for c in confident):
723	            del candidates[k]
724	    if not candidates:
725	        return confident, []
726	
727	    # THE SEMANTIC SECOND STAGE: the contract's definitions, scored against the span's own words.
728	    scores: dict[str, tuple] = {}
729	    for k, _hit in candidates.items():
730	        m = matchers[k]
731	        covered = len(m.vocab & blob_stems) / max(1, len(m.vocab))   # how much of ITS meaning is here
732	        scores[k] = (round(covered, 6), len(m.vocab & blob_stems))
733	    best = max(scores.values())
734	    winners = [k for k, s in scores.items() if s == best]
735	    if len(winners) == 1 and best[0] > 0:
736	        return confident + winners, []
737	    return confident, sorted(candidates)          # a tie is AMBIGUOUS. It is never broken by guessing.
738	
739	
740	def _hits(matchers: dict[str, TermMatcher], blob: str) -> list[str]:
741	    """Back-compat shim: the confidently-routed keys only. Anything that needs to know about
742	    ambiguity must call route_terms() -- a caller that cannot see an ambiguity will report it as a gap.
743	    """
744	    return route_terms(matchers, blob)[0]

763	def has_direct_result(card: dict) -> bool:
764	    """Does this card STATE SOMETHING the source found/held/recommended, rather than name a topic?
765	
766	    A TYPED EVIDENCE ACT IS ALWAYS A RESULT OF ITS TYPE. If the card carries an `act`, it has already
767	    been through that act's required-field rule in the miner's registry gate, against the verbatim
768	    span: a `doctrinal_holding_or_rule` HAS a holding and an authority, or it would not exist.
769	
770	    THIS BRANCH IS LOAD-BEARING AND IT WAS MISSING. The fallback below is `_RESULT_VERB`, and a
771	    judicial holding contains no result verb -- "the employer bears the burden of proving that the
772	    system is job-related" has no `found`, no `showed`, no `increased`. So the miner would mine the
773	    opinion, the gate would admit the holding, the card would be bound and correct -- and the coverage
774	    matrix would score the cell `0 results` and refuse to close it. The evidence would have been
775	    rescued at the extractor and thrown away at the matrix, which is exactly the shape of every defect
776	    in this project's history: a repair that stops one component short of the thing that ships.
777	
778	    The fallback remains for LEGACY, untyped cards, and it is a heuristic, and it is labelled as one.
779	    It decides COVERAGE, never faithfulness.
780	    """
781	    if (card.get('act') or '').strip():
782	        return True
783	    return bool(_RESULT_VERB.search(card.get('span') or ''))

804	def evidence_unit_of(card: dict, graph=None) -> tuple[str, bool]:
805	    """(the INDEPENDENT EVIDENCE-UNIT FAMILY this card belongs to, is_bound).
806	
807	    A family is a STUDY, a DECISION, a TRIAL -- never a document and never a DOI.
808	
809	      scientific / clinical : distinct STUDIES or TRIALS. The NBER working paper and the JPE article
810	                              are TWO EXPRESSIONS OF ONE STUDY, and they are ONE unit. 0.37 vs 0.2 is
811	                              what PEER REVIEW DID TO A NUMBER -- it is A VERSION CHANGE, and it is
812	                              never cross-study corroboration and never a literature conflict.
813	      legal                 : distinct DECISIONS. Two reporters printing one judgment are ONE unit;
814	                              the appellate and the lower-court opinions are SEPARATE units (they are
815	                              separate decisions, and related authority), which falls out for free
816	                              because the graph models them as separate works.
817	
818	    `len(dois)` gets every one of those wrong, in the direction that INFLATES the count -- and an
819	    inflated count is what closes a cell.
820	    """
821	    u = card.get('evidence_unit_id') or card.get('work_id')
822	    if u:
823	        return u, True
824	    if graph is not None:
825	        mid = card.get('manifestation_id')
826	        m = getattr(graph, 'manifestations', {}).get(mid) if mid else None
827	        if m is not None:
828	            return m.work_id, True
829	
830	    # UNBOUND: the card names a DOI and NOTHING THAT RESOLVES TO BYTES. It gets NO FAMILY.
831	    #
832	    # It would be so easy to fall back to the DOI here — the card is not lost, and the count still
833	    # works. THAT FALLBACK IS THE ATTACK. Hand the matrix a working-paper card and a journal-article
834	    # card of ONE STUDY, each with its own DOI and no binding, and `doi:` families count TWO
835	    # independent works and CLOSE THE CELL on one piece of evidence. The adversary's attack 4 does
836	    # exactly this, and it still succeeded against the first version of this function.
837	    #
838	    # A DOI names a WORK and a work has no bytes. It cannot tell two studies from two versions of one,
839	    # so it cannot be an evidence unit, so an unbound card counts toward NOTHING. It is kept, reported
840	    # (Cell.unbound, Matrix.unbound) and it BLOCKS the cell from closing or denying — because evidence
841	    # we hold and cannot ground is not evidence of absence either.
842	    return '', False

287	def _source_policy_from(sp) -> object:
288	    """research_contract.SourcePolicy (what the QUESTION demanded) -> provenance.SourcePolicy (which
289	    EXPRESSIONS a span may therefore name). Two modules, two vocabularies, ONE instruction — and the
290	    translation between them is the only place the task's rule becomes a rule about bytes.
291

293	    it means a span from the NBER working paper MAY NOT BE PRINTED under the JPE's name, however
294	    verbatim the span and however real the DOI.
295	    """
296	    P = prov()
297	    if sp is None:
298	        return P.ANY_VERSION
299	    get = (lambda k: sp.get(k)) if isinstance(sp, dict) else (lambda k: getattr(sp, k, None))
300	    if not get('peer_reviewed_only'):
301	        return P.ANY_VERSION
302	    excluded = [str(x).lower() for x in (get('excluded_types') or [])]

310	    NEVER raises. NEVER blocks. A miner that cannot mine without another agent's module is not a
311	    miner, it is a dependency."""
312	    if facets:
313	        terms = []
314	        for f in facets:
315	            if isinstance(f, dict):
316	                terms.append({'key': f.get('name') or f.get('key') or '',
317	                              'label': f.get('label') or f.get('name') or '',
318	                              'aliases': list(f.get('terms') or f.get('aliases') or [])})
319	            elif isinstance(f, str):
320	                terms.append({'key': f, 'label': f, 'aliases': [f]})
321	        c = MiningContract(question=question, families=[_family('facet', terms)], origin='argument')
322	        log(f'  [contract] {len(terms)} facets passed in by the caller')
323	        return c
324	
325	    if question:
326	        try:
327	            import research_contract as rc  # type: ignore
328	            obj = rc.compile_contract(question, use_llm=True, verbose=False)
329	            c = _contract_from_obj(obj, question)
330	            if c:
331	                log(f'  [contract] research_contract.compile_contract() -> '
332	                    f'{sum(len(f.matchers) for f in c.families)} terms across '
333	                    f'{len(c.families)} families ({", ".join(f.axis for f in c.families)})')
334	                return c
335	        except Exception as e:
336	            log(f'  [contract] research_contract.py did not yield a contract ({type(e).__name__}: {e})')
337	
338	    # The contract cache may ONLY be consulted when we have a question to match it against.
339	    #
340	    # This branch originally read "newest cached contract wins" and the self-test caught it: with no
341	    # question, the miner adopted whichever contract another agent happened to have compiled last —
342	    # for a DIFFERENT research question — and tagged every card against a foreign taxonomy. Silent,
343	    # wrong, and invisible downstream. A contract that was not compiled from THIS question is not
344	    # this question's contract, and no contract at all is strictly better than the wrong one.
345	    cache_dir = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'contracts'
346	    if question and cache_dir.exists():
347	        for p in sorted(cache_dir.glob('*.json'), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True):
348	            try:
349	                obj = json.loads(p.read_text())
350	                if flatten(str(obj.get('question', ''))).lower() != flatten(question).lower():
351	                    continue
352	                c = _contract_from_obj(obj, question)
353	                if c:
354	                    c.origin = f'cache:{p.name}'
355	                    log(f'  [contract] reusing the contract compiled for THIS question ({p.name}, '
356	                        f'{sum(len(f.matchers) for f in c.families)} terms)')
357	                    return c
358	            except Exception:
359	                continue

361	    if question:
362	        words = [w.lower() for w in re.findall(r'[A-Za-z][A-Za-z-]{3,}', question)]
363	        terms = sorted({w for w in words if w not in _STOP})

366	            return MiningContract(question=question, origin='question',
367	                                  families=[_family('topic', [{'key': t, 'label': t, 'aliases': [t]}
368	                                                              for t in terms])])

"""P1 — foundation acceptance battery for the binding gate.

Every vector runs through the REAL chain: migrate() / ingest_bytes -> the ONE identity reducer
(event_ledger.derive_binding_core) -> Graph.resolve_attribution(binding|mid, policy), and where the
disk is the attack surface, through Graph.to_json / Graph.from_json. No Attribution is mocked and no
successful verdict is hand-assigned; the tests only ever READ the structured identity layer
(identity_verdict / disposition / reason_code) the resolver stamps.

Generality (Sol global rule 1 & 6): no rule under test may key on a DOI, title, author, venue, or
subject literal. The metamorphic tests replace every identifier and subject term while holding the
STRUCTURE constant, and assert the structural verdict is unchanged.
"""
import copy

import pytest

import provenance as P
from provenance import (DISPOSITION_ADMIT, DISPOSITION_LEAD_ONLY, DISPOSITION_QUARANTINE,
                        RC_ADMITTED, RC_SPAN_BINDING_INVALID, RC_IDENTITY_DIFFERENT_WORK,
                        RC_IDENTITY_UNRESOLVED, RC_IDENTITY_UNKNOWN_VERDICT, RC_INCOMPLETE_BYTES,
                        RC_VERSION_NOT_PERMITTED)

FILLER = ('This paper studies the question at length across many pages of careful analysis, '
          'reporting the design, the data and the estimates in full. ') * 110


def _row(requested_doi, body_doi, *, title='A Rigorous Study of the Widget Mechanism',
         authors=('Adams', 'Brown'), byline='By Alice Adams and Bob Brown', venue='Journal of Widgets',
         results='We find the effect is 0.2 units (standard error 0.05) across 722 sites.', **extra):
    """A single corpus row whose FULLTEXT carries `body_doi` in its own front matter. The row asks for
    `requested_doi`. When the two agree the bytes are SAME_WORK; when they differ the front matter is a
    foreign DOI and the bytes are a stranger's paper (DIFFERENT_WORK) — positive evidence, both ways."""
    src = (f'{title}\ndoi: {body_doi}\n{byline}\n'
           f'1. Introduction\n{FILLER}\n4. Results\n{results}\n')
    row = {'doi': requested_doi, 'title': title, 'authors': list(authors),
           'venue': venue, 'year': 2020, 'type': 'journal-article', 'fulltext': src, 'abstract': ''}
    row.update(extra)
    return row


def _one(rows):
    g = P.migrate(rows)
    m = next(iter(g.manifestations.values()))
    return g, m


# ── identity: the positive-proof core ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def test_live_foreign_doi_derives_different_work():
    g, m = _one([_row('10.9999/zzzz', '10.2222/bbbb')])
    assert m.profile['semantic_binding'] == 'DIFFERENT_WORK'
    att = g.resolve_attribution(m.id, P.ANY_VERSION)
    assert att.identity_verdict == 'DIFFERENT_WORK'
    assert att.disposition == DISPOSITION_QUARANTINE
    assert att.reason_code == RC_IDENTITY_DIFFERENT_WORK
    assert att.admitted is False
    assert att.names_expression_id is None
    assert att.text is None
    assert att.permitted_expression_ids == ()


def test_matching_doi_is_same_work_and_admits_under_any_version():
    g, m = _one([_row('10.2222/bbbb', '10.2222/bbbb')])
    assert m.profile['semantic_binding'] == 'SAME_WORK'
    att = g.resolve_attribution(m.id, P.ANY_VERSION)
    assert att.admitted is True
    assert att.disposition == DISPOSITION_ADMIT
    assert att.reason_code == RC_ADMITTED
    assert att.names_expression_id is not None


def test_changing_only_requested_doi_rejects_the_same_bytes_under_both_policies():
    body = '10.2222/bbbb'
    # Same bytes, two requested identities. Only the requested DOI differs.
    _, ok = _one([_row(body, body)])
    _, foreign = _one([_row('10.9999/zzzz', body)])
    assert ok.text == foreign.text  # the bytes are byte-identical
    for policy in (P.JOURNAL_ONLY, P.ANY_VERSION):
        g_ok, m_ok = _one([_row(body, body)])
        g_f, m_f = _one([_row('10.9999/zzzz', body)])
